channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Russian warship fires warning shots at UK-flagged yacht in the English Channel
item.description: The British military said it is investigating after a Russian warship fired warning shots at a UK-registered yacht in the English Channel on Tuesday, about 30 kilometres south of the Isle of Wight. Russia said one of its warships fired warning shots after it made a "dangerous approach".
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pont Neuf 'Cavern' installation over the Seine finally opens after damage
item.description: Visitors are finally stepping into darkness at the Pont Neuf, where French artist JR has transformed Paris’s oldest bridge into a towering artificial cavern. The installation was originally set to open to the public on June 6, but strong winds tore parts of the inflatable structure a few days before its début. Combining scent, sound, photography and augmented reality, the free installation invites people to experience one of the city’s most familiar landmarks in an entirely new way.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Super powers in the 21st century 'often not able' to defeat smaller powers
item.description: Haxie Meyers-Belkin is pleased to welcome Peter Zamaya, Director of the Eurasia Democracy Initiative. His central argument is that traditional assumptions about military strength are increasingly being challenged by technological innovation, asymmetric warfare, and political resilience. In this framework, Ukraine's ability to strike deep into Russian territory and sustain resistance against a much larger adversary serves as evidence that military size alone no longer guarantees strategic success. He suggests that the conflict's ultimate resolution will depend less on battlefield maps than on political calculations in Moscow, Washington, and Kyiv, as well as on the ability of the international community to create a durable framework for Ukrainian sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Iran: Uncertainty over Iran deal looms large at G7 summit
item.description:
🇺🇸🇮🇷 The question on everyone’s mind…
Blick's Richard Werly tells #F24Debate uncertainty over the contents of Trump's #Iran agreement looms large at the Évian #G7summit ➡️ https://go.france24.com/vZZZ
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: In post-Assad Syria, displaced people await reconstruction
item.description: Syria has been particularly hard hit by the decline in global humanitarian aid, especially from the US. In the northwest of the country, hundreds of thousands of displaced people are still living in makeshift shelters. When the Assad regime fell, they hoped to be able to return home. But on seeing their villages destroyed, most have remained stranded in camps, waiting for reconstruction that will take years. As time goes by, living conditions there are deteriorating and vital infrastructure such as hospitals are at risk of closing.
item.dc:creator: Dana ALBOZ

item.title: Football fashion: Has France already won?
item.description: In this edition of Entre Nous we take a deep dive into French football fashion. For many, Les Bleus are already the champions of a de facto sartorial competition, particularly on the Clairefontaine "catwalk", when the players head to their training ground. We also discuss the rich history and many rules regarding their FFF, Nike and Jacquemus kits, as fans and collectors are already lining up for the shirts.
item.dc:creator: Solange MOUGIN

item.title: Drone-making workshops take off in Paris
item.description: Are Europeans ready for war? It’s a question that has been asked increasingly often as Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine has dragged on, now lasting longer than the First World War. The threat of future attacks on European soil has prompted a growing interest in new defence technologies – particularly drones. Young people and older generations alike have taken to getting hands-on, attending drone-making workshops, including one which was held in Paris recently.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Graham Coxon on Blur, Brexit and his back catalogue
item.description: In this edition of arts24, we meet a musical polymath and an icon of British popular music. Graham Coxon is one of the founding members of Blur, pioneers of the Britpop movement that dominated the 1990s music scene. A solo artist in his own right, Coxon has released nine studio albums and three soundtracks over the last two decades. Now for the first time, he is re-releasing his entire solo catalogue as well as a new record, "Castle Park", which had been locked away in the musical vaults for more than 15 years. Our Music Editor Jennifer Ben Brahim sat down with him in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer BEN BRAHIM

item.title: 'Belief has plummeted' among Brazil fans after tricky World Cup start
item.description: Before the World Cup began, one in three Brazilians thought their team would lift the trophy, according to one poll. But after struggling to a 1-1 draw against Morocco in their opening match, confidence has plummeted, says France 24's correspondent in Rio Jan Onoszko.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup: How will Deschamps balance attacking options as France take on Senegal?
item.description: France 24's Selina Sykes looks ahead to France's opening match of the 2026 World Cup which will see them taking on Senegal in New York on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Now is the moment': G7 powers push Russia to end Ukraine war
item.description: G7 leaders agreed on Tuesday to intensify pressure on Russia to end more than four years of war against Ukraine, with US President Donald Trump saying Moscow should "make a deal". Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky joined the summit at the French resort of Evian-les-Bains, with Ukraine seen as holding up well on the battlefield but its cities still the target of deadly Russian attacks in a conflict that has now lasted longer than World War I.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Arlindo Chissale, a disappearance in the shadows of Mozambique's security services
item.description: Arlindo Chissale, a journalist and political activist in Mozambique, disappeared without a trace on January 7, 2025, in Cabo Delgado, a region in the grips of an Islamist insurrection. Chissale’s public stance and worrying signs in the days leading up to his disappearance make his loved ones suspect that the Mozambican security forces were involved.
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: Arlindo Chissale, the Mozambican activist and journalist who knew too much
item.description: Arlindo Chissale, a journalist and activist who was in contact with the FRANCE 24 Observers team, disappeared on January 7, 2025, in the region of Cabo Delgado, Mozambique, which is the scene of a deadly Islamist insurrection. Chissale spoke out about topics the Mozambican government did not want out in the open – a pursuit that his friends say put him in danger.
item.dc:creator: The FRANCE 24 Observers

item.title: French department store BHV Marais to end Shein partnership after backlash
item.description: French department store BHV Marais will end its partnership with Shein after its operating company sold the landmark Paris outlet to a group of executives. The move follows months of controversy over the fast-fashion retailer’s presence in the store, including outcry over its environmental impact, labour practices and its online sale of childlike sex dolls.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Who is Didier Deschamps, the France coach aiming for a third World Cup title?
item.description: Didier Deschamps has been coaching the French men's national football team for 14 years, a record period at the helm. He and his players are now aiming for a third World Cup title. The tournament in the US, Canada and Mexico will be his last competition as head of Les Bleus. France in Focus takes a closer look at the man whose name is synonymous with French football success.
item.dc:creator: Aurore Cloé DUPUIS

item.title: French startup unveils non-humanoid robot as AI race moves to physical machines
item.description: French robotics startup Genesis AI on Tuesday unveiled "Eno", its first general-purpose robot, marking a step toward bringing advanced AI from online chatbots into physical machines. Backed by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt, the company says the wheeled robot is designed to extend human capabilities rather than mimic human form, with commercial deployments planned from late 2026.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: How can a joint France-UK mission help restart shipping in the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: A joint France and UK-led naval mission is ready to deploy to the Strait of Hormuz following an interim agreement between the US and Iran to end the Middle East war. Backed by around 20 countries, the mission aims to draw on widespread logistical know-how and resources to help clear mines and escort commercial vessels to restore confidence among shipowners.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: REPLAY: Deal will mean 'Iran will never have a nuclear weapon', says Trump
item.description: US ​President Donald Trump on Tuesday told reporters at the G7 meetings ​in France ‌that the ⁠memorandum of understanding with Iran ‌states clearly that Iran will not ⁠have a nuclear weapon. Trump said that he ​will release the text ‌of the U.S.-Iran memorandum in a formal setting.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Who are the winners and losers of the US-Iran deal?
item.description: After three and a half months of war, the agreement to end the conflict in the Middle East could therefore be signed this Friday in Geneva by the United States and Iran: what does it actually contain? And, above all, who comes out on top after these 108 days of war?
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ukraine 'now has cards to play' as Zelensky meets with Trump
item.description: A year ago at the White House, Donald Trump told Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky that his country had "no cards to play" in negotiating an end to the war with Russia. But, as the two met again at the G7 summit in France on Tuesday, it has become clear the situation has changed, says France 24's Kyiv correspondent Gulliver Cragg.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'The case of Lyhanna is not exceptional': Head of Women for Women France
item.description: The CEO of a French group that fights for the rights of women and children in France has hit out at the French government and justice system. Her comments come after the murder of 11-year-old Lyhanna. The prime suspect in the case is a man who had previously been reported to police as a suspected sex offender. Sarah McGrath of Women for Women France says the case of Lyhanna is unfortunately not exceptional, and shows the typical functioning of the French justice system. She spoke to us in Perspective.
item.dc:creator: Stuart NORVAL

item.title: Western Cape still counting the cost of last month's floods
item.description: In tonight's programme: South Africa's Western Cape is still counting the cost of last month's devastating floods. Also, in DR Congo, the government has reported a substantial daily jump in Ebola cases. Plus Ivorian fans are living large in the wake of their team's victorious first World Cup appearance in 12 years on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: Mass rollout planned across Southern Africa for new HIV drug
item.description: Administered just twice a year, the drug Lenacapavir offers near-complete protection — more than 99% — against HIV. After years of research and clinical trials, the preventive treatment has been approved and is now set to be rolled out on a large scale. Mass rollout campaigns are planned across Southern Africa — South Africa, Zambia, Eswatini, Zimbabwe, Lesotho and Mozambique — the region hardest hit by HIV/AIDS. Our team on the ground reports.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
